security_trigger_reason: F-0029 implements Telegram egress with bot token, operator-only recipient admission, outbound Bot API transport, durable DB evidence, retry behavior, and fake Bot API smoke surfaces.

must_fix: Blocking security/side-effect idempotency issue: telegram.sendMessage transport uses a non-aborting timeout and records timeout as retryable, so a slow Bot API response can still deliver the first message while the outbox is marked retry_scheduled and later sends the same action_id again. Fix by making timeout handling non-duplicating for this protected side effect, for example by aborting transport where possible and treating ambiguous timeout delivery as non-retryable until reconciled, or by adding a durable unknown/lease state that cannot issue a second Bot API send for the same action_id without proof the first request did not reach Telegram.

notes: FAIL: operator admission, caller-recipient refusal, fail-closed config, token sanitization, DB token hygiene, and fake Bot API smoke coverage were reviewed; blocking risk is retrying ambiguous timed-out Telegram sends for the same protected side effect.
